lede: A look at April 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Adolf Hitler and Phil Hill, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Danica Patrick
events.desc: Danica Patrick wins the Indy Japan 300, becoming the first female driver in history to win an Indy car race.

events.title: Enoch Powell
events.desc: English politician Enoch Powell makes his controversial "Rivers of Blood" speech.

events.title: South African Airways Flight 228
events.desc: South African Airways Flight 228 crashes near J.G. Strijdom Airport in South West Africa (now Hosea Kutako International Airport in Namibia), killing 123 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: U.S. troops capture Leipzig, Germany, only to later cede the city to the Soviet Union.

events.title: Soviet Union
events.desc: The Soviet government creates the South Ossetian Autonomous Oblast within the Georgian SSR.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Manfred von Richthofen, a.k.a. The Red Baron, shoots down his 79th and 80th victims, his final victories before his death the following day.

events.title: Louis Pasteur
events.desc: Louis Pasteur and Claude Bernard complete the experiment disproving the theory of spontaneous generation.

events.title: René Caillié
events.desc: René Caillié becomes the second non-Muslim to enter Timbuktu, following Major Gordon Laing. He would also be the first to return alive.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington arrives at Grays Ferry, Philadelphia, while en route to Manhattan for his inauguration.

events.title: Georgia (country)
events.desc: The Georgian king, Erekle II, abandoned by his Russian ally Count Totleben, wins a victory over Ottoman forces at Aspindza.

births.name: Adolf Hitler
births.line: Austrian-born German politician, Führer of Nazi Germany

births.name: Napoleon III
births.line: French politician, 1st President of France, Emperor of French Second Empire

births.name: John Paul Stevens
births.line: American lawyer and jurist, Associate Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States

births.name: Thein Sein
births.line: Burmese general and politician, 8th President of Burma

births.name: Massimo D'Alema
births.line: Italian journalist and politician, 76th Prime Minister of Italy

deaths.text: Bernard Katz, German-English biophysicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
K. Ferdinand Braun, German-American physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Bram Stoker, Anglo-Irish novelist and critic, created Count Dracula
Güyük Khan, Mongol ruler, 3rd Great Khan of the Mongol Empire

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Anastazy Jakub Pankiewicz
Christian feast day: Blessed James Bell and John Finch
Christian feast day: Blessed Muiris Mac Ionrachtaigh
